Commercial Apr 7, 2026

A Look At The Salaries Of Pilots In Canada In 2026

Diving deeper into the economics of professional flying in the Great White North.

Canadian pilot salaries are increasingly competitive as regional carriers raise pay to address staffing shortages amid economic recovery and international recruitment.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Starfish Space raises more than $100 million

Starfish Space has raised more than $100 million to scale up production of its satellite servicing spacecraft.

Investor confidence in on-orbit servicing validates a new market segment that could extend satellite lifespans and reduce orbital debris, creating operational opportunities for launch and ground support services.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.

Newly anointed NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman made a $20 billion announcement on March 24, 2026. To the delight of space enthusiasts everywhere, Isaacman said NASA was cancelling its project to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and would reorient to building a $20 billion base on the surface...

NASA's shift from orbital to surface-based lunar infrastructure significantly increases operational complexity and cybersecurity risks for critical space systems supporting long-term human presence.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

First refurbished Jetstar 787 Dreamliner takes flight from Melbourne

Jetstar’s first refurbished Boeing 787 Dreamliner launched its inaugural service from Melbourne Airport (MEL) to Phuket International Airport…...

Jetstar's 787 deployment signals budget carriers' shift toward wide-body aircraft for long-haul routes, potentially lowering trans-Pacific fares through increased low-cost capacity.

See AeroTime Hub →

Nominate space industry leaders for the 2026 SpaceNews Icon Awards

Tell us about the innovators, collaborators and change-makers whose contributions have left a lasting mark on the sector...

SpaceNews Icon Awards recognize transformational figures who shape space industry strategy, providing visibility for leaders influencing aerospace sector evolution.

Regulatory Apr 7, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS transforms aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating risks before incidents happen.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

British Airways Flew 13 Hours After Passenger Died Onboard - Foul Smell Complaints Followed

You might expect that, when a passenger passes away during a flight, pilots would divert the aircraft to an airport to deal with the situation. However, this isn’t always the case. Indeed, if a passenger dies of natural causes, airlines may just opt to have the flight continue onward to its...

Airlines operating under current protocols can legally continue flights after passenger deaths from natural causes, creating operational and customer experience management challenges for carriers.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

Up To 16-Hour Nonstop Flights: Iberia's 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Influenced by external factors, one very long link takes the crown, but what about the rest?

Iberia's expansion into ultra-long-haul represents a strategic pivot toward point-to-point connectivity that could reduce hub dependence and capture premium transoceanic demand.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

The Problem With Replacing Aircraft Like The Boeing 747

The biggest problem with replacing the Boeing 747 is simple: no single aircraft can do everything the 747 did.

The 747's versatility across cargo, long-haul passenger, and specialized missions created an irreplaceable operational niche that forces airlines to deploy multiple aircraft types instead of one workhorse.

See Simple Flying →

Part 5 compliance requires systematic risk assessment methodologies that go beyond traditional compliance checklists to identify organizational safety vulnerabilities.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Earth observation operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes

Vantor employees were gathered for a sales kickoff in January, when an executive announced that a WorldView Legion satellite passing overhead would snap a photo of the California venue. Later, a buzzer sounded to alert the audience that the 30-centimeter-resolution image was available on the Vantor...

Real-time Earth observation imagery is becoming commercially viable, enabling immediate tactical applications that previously required hours of processing and delivery.

See SpaceNews →

Military Apr 7, 2026

YFQ-42A Dark Merlin CCA Crashes in California During Test

GA-ASI confirmed a YFQ-42 crashed during take-off and testing has now been paused temporarily, with an investigation already launched. A prototype of the YFQ-42A Dark Merlin Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA), developed by General Atomics Aeronautical Systems, Inc (GA-ASI) crashed at the...

The YFQ-42A crash suspension pauses development of a key unmanned combat aircraft program during critical testing phases, potentially impacting DoD's collaborative warfare strategy.

See The Aviationist →

Corporate Apr 7, 2026

Air India CEO Campbell Wilson resigns, will stay until successor named

Air India CEO and Managing Director Campbell Wilson announced his resignation on April 7, 2026, ending a nearly four-year tenure…...

Wilson's departure marks a critical transition for Air India as it undergoes fleet modernization and competitive repositioning in a recovering post-pandemic market.

See AeroTime Hub →

Regulatory Apr 7, 2026

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise procedures must account for surrounding terrain and proximity to SFO Class B airspace, creating unique operational constraints for departure planning.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Military Apr 7, 2026

South Korean helicopter accidentally crossed into DMZ while fighting wildfire

A South Korean Army Surion helicopter mistakenly flew into the Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) separating North and South Korea…...

Operational coordination failures during emergency responses highlight critical gaps in real-time airspace management between military and civilian fire-suppression units near sensitive borders.

See AeroTime Hub →

Military Apr 7, 2026

Australia’s JORN radar set to power Canada’s Arctic defense ambitions

Australia’s Jindalee Operational Radar Network is set to help Canada build the biggest and most advanced over-the-horizon radar…...

Australia's proven JORN technology transfer to Canada demonstrates how allied nations are leveraging existing radar capabilities to rapidly modernize Arctic surveillance infrastructure against emerging threats.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Apr 7, 2026

Manna expands drone delivery network with $50M boost

Ireland-based drone delivery company Manna Air Delivery is making a serious push into the US, and it just secured the funding to back it up. The company announced a fresh $50 million Series B round, bringing its total funding to $110 million, as it prepares to rapidly scale operations across the...

Manna's $50M Series B signals investor confidence that international drone operators can compete in the fragmented US market despite regulatory complexity.

See DroneDJ →

Safety Apr 7, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Helicopter pilots systematically underestimate wire strike risk due to false confidence in reaction time, making proactive route planning and terrain awareness training critical safety imperatives.

Delta's Massive Growth Continues: Record Flights On 3 New Long-Haul Routes

The airline solidifies its position in the transatlantic market, catering to the growing demand for direct flights to these destinations.

Delta's expansion into underserved long-haul markets indicates airlines are shifting capacity from domestic routes to capitalize on higher-margin international travel recovery.

Military Apr 7, 2026

General Atomics YFQ-42A drone crashes during California test flight

A General Atomics YFQ-42A Collaborative Combat Aircraft prototype crashed shortly after takeoff in the California desert on April…...

The YFQ-42A crash highlights developmental risks in autonomous combat systems that could influence DoD acquisition timelines for collaborative warfare platforms.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation segments offers a more immediate pathway to emissions reduction than waiting for fleet-wide aircraft replacement.

See MDPI →

Drones Apr 7, 2026

DJI confirms end-of-support timeline for Mavic 2, Matrice 600 drones

Tech giant DJI will officially end service and support for several of its most beloved professional and prosumer drones in the coming weeks. That includes the iconic DJI Mavic 2 Pro, the versatile DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Zoom and DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Dual, and the heavy-lift powerhouse DJI Matrice...

DJI's support discontinuation for legacy Mavic 2 and Matrice 600 lines forces professional operators to plan equipment refreshes and evaluate platform migration strategies.

See DroneDJ →

Northrop Grumman’s Lumberjack Advances Battlefield Capabilities

Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) showcased the autonomous mission capabilities of its Lumberjack™ Group 3 Uncrewed Aircraft...

Lumberjack's autonomous capabilities reduce pilot workload while enabling simultaneous multi-vehicle coordination, increasing operational effectiveness in contested environments.

A couple and their newborn are removed from the plane.

Airlines' reliance on third-party medical advisors for passenger removal decisions creates liability and reputational risks when protocols lack transparency or appear disproportionate to actual safety threats.

Iran War Fallout: Airlines Slash Thousands Of Flights Amid Global Fuel Shortages

As the Iran crisis escalates, airlines worldwide are grappling with skyrocketing fuel costs and limited access to jet fuel.

Geopolitical disruptions are triggering cascading supply chain failures across the airline industry, forcing carriers to reduce capacity rather than absorb unsustainable fuel cost increases.

Always trying to resolve issues before they start.

Flight crews employ de-escalation protocols and early intervention to prevent disruptions rather than simply reacting to incidents, reducing operational delays and safety risks.

General Aviation Apr 7, 2026

Drought Parches Florida

The state was unusually dry for much of 2025, but the intensity of the drought has ratcheted up since January 2026.

Prolonged drought conditions in Florida may impact airport operations, water availability for aircraft de-icing, and runway maintenance schedules for regional aviation hubs.

See NASA →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Artemis 2 swings around the moon

Four astronauts from the United States and Canada became the humans to travel the furthest from the Earth April 6 as they went around the moon on the Artemis 2 mission.

Artemis 2's successful lunar flyby validates NASA's deep-space architecture for sustained human exploration beyond Earth orbit.

Why Air New Zealand is unable to compete on its flagship route.

Ultra long-haul viability hinges on aircraft economics and crew fatigue regulations that constrain frequency and load factors beyond published schedules.

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight

Editor’s note: This release was updated at 7:28 p.m. EDT on April 6, 2026, to reflect Orion’s final closest approach to the lunar surface from about 4,070 miles to about 4,067 miles. NASA also updated the farthest distance Orion will travel. Finally, NASA also made changes at 10 p.m. to the...

Artemis II's lunar trajectory demonstrates NASA's validation of deep-space navigation and life-support systems critical for sustainable human exploration beyond Earth orbit.

See NASA →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview

NASA’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24, will deliver approximately 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station. This mission will be the second flight of the Cygnus XL, the larger, more cargo-capable version of the...

Cygnus XL's increased capacity to 11,000 pounds represents a significant enhancement to ISS resupply logistics, reducing mission frequency requirements and improving cost-efficiency for sustained orbital operations.

See NASA →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

K-State Salina Launches High School Aviation Camp

Weeklong program will include simulator work, ground school and up to three hours of flight time.

High school aviation camps with integrated flight hours accelerate pilot certification pathways while building early interest in addressing aviation's persistent pilot shortage.
